channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Teenager faces legal action after speeding 70kmh over parkway limit
item.description: A 17-year-old P-plater has had their licence suspended and vehicle seized after being caught speeding at 170kmh on the Tuggeranong Parkway, 70kmh over the posted limit. Police stopped the driver follo...

item.title: Tiger Beer Launches Lighter Version to Attract Younger Audience
item.description: Tiger Beer, a popular Nigerian brand, has relaunched its product with a lighter, smoother taste and refreshed packaging to attract younger consumers. The new version, called Tiger 4.0, features a refo...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Condemns US Breaches of Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has strongly criticized the United States for repeatedly breaching a ceasefire agreement, calling President Donald Trump's signature on the deal 'utterly w...

item.title: Fish Hoek Surf Lifesaving Club Marks 70 Years of Service and Success
item.description: The Fish Hoek Surf Lifesaving Club, founded in 1958 by seven Sea Scouts including Clive Duffell, has grown from humble beginnings to become one of South Africa's most successful surf lifesaving organi...

item.title: Iran and US Escalate Gulf Conflict with Attacks and Casualties
item.description: Iran has intensified attacks on US military targets in the Gulf region, resulting in the deaths of two American personnel and one missing in action. The conflict follows the collapse of an interim cea...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates with military strikes and diplomatic tensions
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified, with significant military actions and diplomatic tensions escalating. At least two US service members were killed in Jordan while defen...

item.title: Meerut Murder Case: Victim Drugged and Killed by Snake in Premeditated Attack
item.description: In Uttar Pradesh's Meerut, a premeditated murder case has shocked the region. Atul Kumar Panwar, a school operator, was found dead in his rented home after being allegedly drugged with sleeping pills ...

item.title: U.S. and Iran escalate conflict with airstrikes and casualties following Jordanian base attack
item.description: The U.S. military has conducted new airstrikes in the Middle East following Iranian attacks on a military base in Jordan, resulting in the deaths of two troops and the disappearance of one. The confli...

item.title: Ryan Fox's Remarkable 62 at Royal Birkdale Sparks Major Championship Contention
item.description: New Zealand golfer Ryan Fox made headlines by shooting a historic third-round 62 at the British Open, matching the lowest round in men's major championship history. The 39-year-old, who started the da...

item.title: TZ-Germany Legal Aid Initiatives Benefit 5 Million Tanzanians
item.description: The article highlights the impact of legal aid initiatives between Tanzania and Germany, which have successfully provided assistance to over five million Tanzanians. These programs aim to improve acce...

item.title: Paul Kelly's Analysis of Liberal Party's Leadership Shifts and Identity Crisis
item.description: In a detailed discussion, political editor Tom McIlroy converses with Paul Kelly about his new book, 'The Twilight of Exceptionalism,' which examines the turbulent period of the Liberal Party under pr...

item.title: Afghanistan-Pakistan Transit Trade Plummets to Historic Low Amid Economic Shifts
item.description: Afghanistan-Pakistan transit trade has experienced one of its steepest declines, dropping to 11,592 containers worth $367 million in FY26 from nearly 89,000 containers valued at $5 billion before the ...

item.title: Delta State's 2027 Election: Criticizing Misplaced Priorities and Push for Industrialization
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing criticisms against the current Delta State Governor's administration, highlighting misplaced priorities in funding projects. While the Governor announced plans to bui...

item.title: Study links gut bacteria imbalance to breast cancer spread and identifies potential treatment
item.description: A groundbreaking study suggests that inexpensive drugs already in use by the NHS could play a pivotal role in preventing the spread of breast cancer. Researchers at the University of Virginia have dis...

item.title: Miss Globe PH 2026 Sasha-Juli Belle Lacuna Inspired by Catriona Gray's Lucky Charm
item.description: The article discusses Sasha-Juli Belle Lacuna, the reigning Miss Globe PH 2026, who credits her success to the 'lucky charm' of former beauty queen Catriona Gray. Published on July 19, 2026, the piece...

item.title: Trump Administration's Crackdown on Far-Left Movements Draws Comparisons to Historical COINTELPRO Tactics
item.description: The article analyzes the Trump administration's intensified efforts against what it labels 'far-left terrorism,' drawing parallels to the FBI's 1950s Counter Intelligence Program (COINTELPRO). Writer ...

item.title: Iran Retaliates Against US Attacks, Killing Two Troops in Gulf Region
item.description: Iran has launched retaliatory strikes against US military targets in the Gulf, killing two US troops and injuring several others. The attacks come after a month of escalating tensions between Iran and...

item.title: Study raises concerns about dangerous nicotine levels in black market vapes
item.description: A recent study has revealed alarming findings about the nicotine content in certain black market vaping products, with some containing levels equivalent to 600 traditional cigarettes. Published on Jul...

item.title: England Clinch Third Place with Dominant 6-4 Win Over France in Chaotic World Cup Bronze Match
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup third-place playoff, England defeated France 6-4 in Miami, marking their best-ever World Cup finish since 1966. The match saw Kylian Mbappe become the all-time top scorer with ...

item.title: Unifor and Ford Announce Tentative Labour Agreement with Wage Increases and Facility Investments
item.description: Unifor, the Canadian labor union, has released details of a proposed tentative agreement with Ford Motor Company, which could secure three per cent annual wage increases for workers over the next thre...
